sections.paragraph:
αἰδώς, οῦς, ἡ (etym. s. end of entry in DGE; Hom.+; Epict.; OGI 507, 8; PCairMasp 295 III, 22 [V a.d.]; LXX; TestJud 14:7; Jos., Bell. 2, 325; SibOr 1, 35; Ath. 30, 2). This term expresses the opposite of considering or treating someth. in a common or ordinary manner; a respect for convention.

① modesty of women (Diod S 13, 55, 4). With σωφροσύνη: μετὰ αἰ. 1 Ti 2:9 (cp. Jos., Ant. 2, 52; AcPl Ox 6 [Aa I 241, 15]).

② reverence, respect (Pind. et al.; Plut., Timol. 238 [7, 1]; Jos., Ant. 6, 262; Iren. 1, 8, 2 [Harv. I 70, 1] w. εὐλάβεια [as Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 352; Ath. 30, 2]) Hb 12:28 v.l. (cp. Appian, Bell. Civ. 1, 71 §331 αἰδὼς θεῶν).—RSchultz, ΑΙΔΩΣ, diss. Rostock 1910; CEvErffa, ΑΙΔΩΣ: Philol. Suppl. 30, 2, ’37; DCairns, Aidōs ’93.—B. 1141. DELG s.v. αἴδομαι. M-M (no pap). TW. Spicq.

etym. etym. = etymology, -ical

DGE DGE = Diccionario Griego-Español—List 6

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AcPl Ox 6 AcPl Ox 6 = Acts of Paul, POxy 6—List 1

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

diss. diss. = dissertation

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 24
